chapter_title.ar: باب الْخُشُوعِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ وَ كَرَاهِيَةِ الْعَبَثِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 16 – The humbleness during the Salāt and abhorrence of the frivolities

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/77)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/77)
text: إِذَا كُنْتَ دَخَلْتَ فِي صَلَاتِكَ فَعَلَيْكَ بِالتَّخَشُّعِ وَ الْإِقْبَالِ عَلَى صَلَاتِكَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَقُولُ الَّذِينَ هُمْ فِي صَلاتِهِمْ خاشِعُونَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Whenever you enter into your Salāt, so upon you is to be with the humbleness and the devotion upon your Salāt, for Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic is Saying [23:2] Who are humble in their Prayers’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that when you are in your Salah (prayer), you must be humble and attentive in it; Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, has said, ‘Those who are humble during their Salah (prayer) (are of the true believers)’ [23:3].”
